title: Local names - non-alphanumeric characters
description:
Query:
PREFIX : <http://www.uochb.org/chebi/1.0/terms#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?S FROM <http://localhost:8890/chebi_db> WHERE
{
    ?X :chebi\=Name / :nameValue ?S.
}

Result:
Virtuoso 37000 Error SP030: SPARQL compiler, line 0: Bad character '\' (0x5c) in SPARQL expression at '\'

But according to SPARQL 1.1 domentation (http://www.w3.org/TR/sparql11-query/#prefNames):
SPARQL local names also allow the non-alphanumeric characters allowed in IRIs via backslash character escapes (e.g. ns:id\=123).
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE chebi_terms (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, value INTEGER DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO chebi_terms (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'chebi=Name', 123);
INSERT INTO chebi_terms (id, name, value) VALUES (2, 'chebi=Description', NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM chebi_terms WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM chebi_terms WHERE name LIKE 'chebi=%';
key_bug_features: Backslash character escape in SPARQL local names
